scheme_name: Aaloo Ke Kufri Chipsona-1 Prabhed Ke Kshetra Vistaar Yojana
page_title: Aaloo Ke Kufri Chipsona-1 Prabhed Ke Kshetra Vistaar Yojana

details: “Aaloo Ke Kufri Chipsona-1 Prabed Ke Kshetra Vistaar Yojana” launched by the Department of Agriculture, Bihar, aims to increase the production of Kufri Chipsona variety suitable for potato processing in Bihar by expanding its area and to increase the income of farmers by promoting processing. Under this scheme, certified quality seeds of Kufri Chipsona variety suitable for potato processing will be made available to farmers.
benefits: Farmers will receive Kufri Chipsona-I potato seeds at an estimated price of ₹4,000/- per quintal or the actual seed price, whichever is lower.
Total ₹93,863/- per hectare (First year: ₹70,397/- and Second year: ₹23,466/-).
eligibility: The applicant should be a resident of Bihar.
The applicant should be a farmer.
The applicant must have agricultural land a minimum of 0.25 acres (0.1 ha) and a maximum of 5 acres (2 ha).
The applicant must be registered on the DBT portal.
The benefits of this scheme can be taken by ryot farmers on the basis of land documents and non-ryot farmers on the basis of contract form.
Beneficiaries will be selected on first come first serve basis.

online: Select Aadhaar verification type: OTP/ BIO-AUTH/ IRIS.
Enter your Aadhaar number and Name as per Aadhaar. Click on “Authentication”.
Enter the OTP. Click on “Validate OTP”.
Now confirm the details about the “Kisan Credit Card (KCC)” Details. Click on “Farmer Registration”.
In the Registration Page, fill in all the details properly i.e. Farmer details, Land information, Bank account details and click on “Submit”.
Validate with OTP. Click on “Register” to generate the Registration Id.
After 48 hours of registration, farmers can apply for the scheme with the help of 13 digit registration number.
Scroll to bottom and confirm the checkbox. Click on “Proceed to apply”.
In the next page select the “Type of Applicant” and enter the “DBT Registration Number of Kisan”. Click on “Get details”.
Fill in the required details i.e. Address, Land Details, etc, and upload documents.
Confirm the checkbox and then click on “Submit”.
An “Application Number” will be generated. Keep this for future reference.
Printout of the filled in application also can be taken using the Print option.

documents_required: Aadhaar Details (Should be linked with mobile number).
For the Online Application of the Scheme:
If the applicant's name is not clear in the land-ownership/ revenue receipt, it will be mandatory to attach the genealogy along with the land-ownership/ revenue receipt.

faqs.question: What is the primary goal of this scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme aims to promote the production of the Kufri Chipsona-I potato variety suitable for processing, thereby increasing farmers’ incomes and supporting the potato processing industry in Bihar.

faqs.question: What benefits do farmers receive under this scheme?
faqs.answer: Farmers receive high-quality Kufri Chipsona-I potato seeds at a subsidized cost and financial assistance totaling ₹93,863/- per hectare over two years.

faqs.question: What is the cost of the potato seeds provided under this scheme?
faqs.answer: The seeds are provided at an estimated cost of ₹4,000/- per quintal or the actual market price, whichever is lower.

faqs.question: What financial assistance is offered to farmers under this scheme?
faqs.answer: Farmers are provided ₹70,397/- per hectare in the first year and ₹23,466/- per hectare in the second year, totaling ₹93,863 per hectare.

faqs.question: Who is eligible to apply for this scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme is open to farmers residing in Bihar who own or lease agricultural land measuring between 0.25 acres and 5 acres and are registered on the DBT portal.

faqs.question: Can non-landholding farmers apply for this scheme?
faqs.answer: Yes, non-landholding farmers (non-ryot) can apply based on contract farming agreements.

faqs.question: How are beneficiaries selected under this scheme?
faqs.answer: Beneficiaries are selected on a first-come, first-served basis, subject to the availability of funds and resources.

faqs.question: Is there any reservation or category-based allocation in the scheme?
faqs.answer: Yes, the scheme allocates benefits as follows: General Category (78.56%), Scheduled Castes (20%), Scheduled Tribes (1.44%), and Women (30%).

faqs.question: What happens if the applicant's name is unclear in land documents?
faqs.answer: If the name is unclear in land-ownership or revenue receipts, the applicant must attach a genealogy document along with the land-related papers.

faqs.question: Do farmers need to register for this scheme?
faqs.answer: Yes, farmers must register on the DBT portal to apply for the scheme.

faqs.question: What type of seeds are provided under the scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme provides certified, high-quality Kufri Chipsona-I seeds suitable for potato processing.

faqs.question: Can women farmers apply for the scheme?
faqs.answer: Yes, 30% of the scheme's benefits are reserved for women farmers.
